title: UK Date Format options
excerpt: Hi there, is it possible, if not already, for the date column to reflect the day as dd/mm/yyyy with the option to include a three letter day tag?

posts.author: kstirzaker (Kevin Stirzaker)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi there, is it possible, if not already, for the date column to reflect the day as dd/mm/yyyy with the option to include a three letter day tag?

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @kstirzaker ! It is possible to adjust the date format via your profile:

Screen Shot 2023-02-06 at 3.01.55 pm1230×834 33.3 KB

It is also possible to include the 3 letter day tag via the date column settings:

Screen Shot 2023-02-06 at 3.02.37 pm720×564 41.4 KB

I hope this helps!

posts.author: kstirzaker (Kevin Stirzaker)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you so much, I almost feel embarrassed at not spotting this. My date column now reads just the way I want it.

However, I need to combine two fields (one for Sale Description and one for Date of Visit) for input into a third party accounting system. Unfortunately, the Board puts the date back into the US format of yyyy-mm-dd . If anybody has any ideas on resolving this aspect I would be so grateful.

Nevertheless, many thanks for the original response.

Kev

posts.author: Kofi (Kofi FiftyTechSolutions.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey Kevin,

Use the formula column, and enter the formula,

FORMAT_DATE({DATE},"DD/MM/YYYY")

Where If the Date in the date column is 8th March 2026, the formula column will show as 08/03/2026.

FORMAT_DATE({DATE},"Do MMM YYYY”)

Would ouput a date as: 8th Mar 2026

Hopefully this helps!
